assertions.expectation: Explains the difference between Rapid and Stable channels — including that Rapid has no SLA for upgrade stability while Stable carries a full SLA, and clearly distinguishes minor version vs patch version differences between the channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains that Rapid has 'No SLA for stability' while Stable has 'Full SLA', and discusses timing differences for patches and versions between channels

assertions.expectation: Notes that channel changes don't trigger an immediate upgrade or downgrade
assertions.reason: Response implicitly demonstrates this understanding by discussing scenarios where cluster becomes 'ahead of channel' and won't receive auto-upgrades until Stable catches up

assertions.expectation: Warns that if 1.30 isn't yet available on Stable, the cluster may not receive patches until Stable catches up
assertions.reason: Response explicitly warns that if 1.32 isn't available in Stable, the cluster will become 'ahead of channel' and receive 'NO auto-upgrades (including security patches) until Stable's version reaches 1.32'

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact command: 'gcloud container clusters update YOUR_CLUSTER --zone YOUR_ZONE --release-channel stable'

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking version availability on Stable before switching (gcloud container get-server-config)
assertions.reason: Response provides the specific command 'gcloud container get-server-config --zone YOUR_ZONE --format="yaml(channels)"' and emphasizes checking version availability first

assertions.expectation: Discusses the impact on auto-upgrade timing going forward
assertions.reason: Response discusses how upgrades arrive weeks later on Stable, security patch delays (2-4 weeks), and the importance of maintenance windows for ongoing operations

assertions.expectation: Mentions that the Extended channel is an option if they need even slower cadence (with cost implications)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the Extended channel as an alternative option for even slower upgrade cadence

assertions.expectation: Advises coordinating the change during a maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response recommends applying temporary upgrade freeze during transition and provides commands for maintenance exclusions, plus discusses configuring maintenance windows
